Note :- if a rack is new and have no mgen software . you need to setup everything
from scratch. for that you can follow the doc given below

https://pages.github.ibm.com/cpsdi/ironphoenix/html/How_to_Build_an_ MGEN _ Laptop.
html

If you are unable to ssh into mgen rack , try pinging the ip
if that too doesn't work,

check if you can ping gateway e.g 9.42.45.1 (mgen ip with last digit 1) if you can ping gateway,
then, most of times, after 2 minutes, it should be back because we do experience network drop
from time to time.. most of times, after 2 minutes, it should recover

if that too not work means rack is down and you need to contact with the Lab (chai,paul)

10.Stage1 failure check
if stage fails at 0% after trying at least 2 times( sometimes on retry it passed)

check if provisioner is able to ping dhcp server
vi /etc/resolv.conf , from there you will get the gateway of dhcp server
try pinging that ip from provisioner(rhel_ru23/rhel_ru7) , if not pinging

then check if dhcp and dns service is running or not by using command(this command
should be run in mgen laptop not in provisioner(rhel_ruX))

systemctl status dhcpd.service ( for dhcp)
systemctl status named.service ( for dns)

if not running , execute above command with restart inplace of status and check again it starts
or not

In Mgen laptop, DHCP file is under /etc/dhcp/dhcpd.conf
for DNS forward and reverse lookup file is in
/var/named/zonefile.db (forward lookup)

/var/named/reverse.db (reverse lookup)

Note:- if you dont find these files in your Mgen laptop rack thats means that rack is not
dhcp server of itself and you have to connect with LAB people(chai/paul )
